Hi, I'm Ishan.

Twitter Email Scholar Code

About

I'm a second-year PhD student at Berkeley AI Research, advised by Jennifer Listgarten. I work on generative models and agents for scientific discovery.I am also an EECS Peer and help run the BAIR Blog.

I spent part of my gap year at Aviv Regev's group at Genentech, working on multimodal language models with the wonderful Yongju Lee. Before that, I graduated from Stanford with bachelor and master degrees in computer science and electrical engineering. During my time there, I was fortunate to have worked with Emma Lundberg on vision models for mapping dynamic changes in the human proteome. I also had a blast working on using new hardware accelerators to run machine learning algorithms in computer networks, advised by Kunle Olukotun and Muhammad Shahbaz.

What's New?

  • [4/27] Announced ProteinGen's private beta during Jenn's talk at the Broad. ProteinGen is a package for library design with machine learning. It focuses on leveraging assay-labeled data to improve libraries sampled using protein sequence models. It makes it super easy to try the latest library design techniques with any of the most common sequences generative models. (We even give you a dead-simple interface for using ProteinMPNN!)
  • [2/23] Bear Xiong and I give a talk on ProteinGuide at Profluent
